sourceSummary.oneLine: Material Safety Data Sheet for XLIM polyolefin foam manufactured by Sekisui Chemical Co., Ltd.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports safety, handling, storage, transport, disposal, and applicable-law documentation for XLIM material.
keyPoints: XLIM is described as a mixture chemical, polyolefin foam, with polyolefin content more than 90%.
The sheet states no hazardous components and no anticipated health influence in ordinary use or processing.
Storage falls under Fire Defense Law as Designated Combustible Material, with notification required for storage or handling of 20 cubic meters.
Disposal by incineration or land filling should comply with Waste Management and Public Cleaning Law, Air Pollution Control Law, and relevant regulations.
UN Classification No. is stated as None.

evidence.claim: The document is a Material Safety Data Sheet for product XLIM.
evidence.evidenceText:
Product： XLIM
Sekisui Chemical Co., Ltd
Material Safety Data Sheet

evidence.claim: The MSDS reference number is XLIM-EⅢ.
The document was edited on 6/5/2007 and revised on 1/3/2019.
The manufacturer is Foam Division, Sekisui Chemical Co., Ltd., located at 2-3-27 Toranomon, Minato-ku, Tokyo, Japan.
evidence.evidenceText:
Manufacturer ： Foam Division, Sekisui Chemical Co., Ltd.
Address ： 2-3-27 Toranomon, Minato-ku, Tokyo, Japan

evidence.claim: XLIM is designated as polyolefin foam and described as a mixture chemical.
evidence.evidenceText:
Nature of composition ： Mixture Chemical
designation ： Polyolefin foam

evidence.claim: The component content is polyolefin more than 90%.
The document states XLIM has no hazardous components under the classification standard.
evidence.evidenceText: Hazardous components ： none (not classified as hazardous in the classification standard)

evidence.claim: Ordinary use or processing is not anticipated to influence health.
evidence.evidenceText: Health hazard ： No influence on health is anticipated in ordinary use or processing.

evidence.claim: The product is combustible and may generate carbon monoxide or hydrocarbons by combustion or thermal decomposition.
evidence.evidenceText: Physical & chemical hazard ： It is combustible and may generate hazardous carbon monoxide or hydrocarbons by combustion or thermal decomposition.

evidence.claim: Storage of XLIM is regulated as Designated Combustible Material under Fire Defense Law.
evidence.evidenceText: Storage ： Fire Defense Law (Designated Combustible Material)

evidence.claim: For storage or handling of 20 cubic meters of the product, notification to the controlling Fire Department is required.
evidence.evidenceText: For storage or handling of 20 cu. Meters of the product, a notification to the controlling Fire Department is required.

evidence.claim: Disposal should comply with Waste Management and Public Cleaning Law, Air Pollution Control Law, and relevant regulations.
evidence.evidenceText: Recycling is possible but disposal by incineration or land filling should comply with ‘Waste Management and Public Cleaning Law’, ‘Air Pollution Control Law’ and relevant regulations.

evidence.claim: The document states UN Classification No. is None.
The document states polyethylene carcinogenicity is IARC Group 3.
evidence.evidenceText: Carcinogenicity ： IARC shares polyethylene as Group 3.

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata productSignal is HAD/A/F 1417-1717, but the source text identifies the product only as XLIM.
No external standard number is identified in the source text.
Applicable laws are named, but the issuing authority is not explicitly stated in the source text.
